pages.title: Accelerators
pages.note:
This is the demand engine's silicon — [[nvda]] plus the challengers. Little new standalone signal this week; the names mostly appeared inside the indiscriminate-selloff list ([[amd]] and peers down 4-10% in the Samsung-triggered rout), which [[aleabitoreddit]] reads as margin cascades rather than fundamentals. He treats [[nvda]] as the gravitational center rather than a pick — the edge is in the layers beneath it ([[photonics]], [[memory]], [[fabs]]).

[[amd]] is the merchant-GPU alternative; [[arm]] the architecture licensor riding the same compute wave. The desk's stance on the group is deliberately hands-off: express the view through the suppliers this map surfaces, not the headline chip.

pages.title: Fabs & Foundries
pages.note:
The fabrication and equipment layer was ground zero for the week's contagion — Samsung's guidance triggered a rout in which [[intc]] was the worst S&P 500 performer and the equipment names sold off hard. [[aleabitoreddit]]'s read is consistent with his cross-sector frame: indiscriminate selling into a leverage-driven tape while the fundamentals (record memory profits, sold-out capacity) held.

[[tsm]] is the gravitational center of the layer; [[gfs]] and [[tsem]] the specialty-foundry expressions; [[intc]] the turnaround/optionality name. The layer gates everything downstream — the [[accelerators]] that [[nvda]] designs are only as available as the fab and [[advanced-packaging]] capacity behind them.

pages.title: Glass Substrates
pages.note:
The newest and thinnest entry in this cycle: glass-core substrates for [[advanced-packaging]], a natural extension of the packaging wave as chips grow larger and run hotter. No dedicated glass-substrate thesis has been posted yet — Corning (GLW) has surfaced only inside a broader Bloomberg-driven semis-selloff basket, and is tracked here as a candidate alongside SKC Absolics and TSMC's CoPoS effort.

The better-developed way [[aleabitoreddit]] plays the same substrate shift lives on the [[materials]] page, via LPKF ([[lpk-de]])'s glass-core equipment near-monopoly. Watch this theme for a first dedicated thesis.

pages.title: Hyperscalers
pages.note:
The buyers of compute — and the center of the week's narrative war. [[aleabitoreddit]] framed the selloff as misinformation: a 1 July Bloomberg piece reported [[meta]] will *sell* its excess compute (becoming a [[neocloud]] competitor), which algorithms misread as capex-cutting. His long-standing 'Meta is not cutting' thesis got public support when Meta's 'Watermelon' model reportedly caught up to GPT 5.5 using an order of magnitude more compute.

[[meta]] is the contested name; [[amzn]], [[googl]] and [[msft]] the other three legs of demand. Tracking them here is directional, not for the mega-caps themselves — hyperscaler capex is the tide that lifts the [[neoclouds]], [[photonics]] and [[memory]] suppliers downstream.

pages.title: Materials & Packaging
pages.note:
A lighter-touch, picks-and-shovels research theme sitting upstream of the [[fabs]] and [[advanced-packaging]] layers. [[aleabitoreddit]] views AEHR ([[aehr]])'s ~100% rally as largely priced in absent a new catalyst; LPKF ([[lpk-de]]) is his recurring bullish idea on a glass-core substrate near-monopoly — a bridge into the [[glass]] theme — with a stated multi-billion factory ambition; Soitec ([[soi-pa]]) rounds out the specialty-materials group.

These are second-derivative plays: they win if the [[memory]], [[photonics]] and packaging build-outs they supply keep scaling.

pages.title: AI Memory
pages.note:
The [[hbm]] memory upcycle is a high-conviction supply theme, and it got a clean real-world stress test this week: Samsung pre-announced record Q2 operating profit up ~1,800% Y/Y — on his telling the most profitable quarter in the world — and the stock still fell ~7% after a +150% YTD run. [[aleabitoreddit]] reads that as priced-to-perfection profit-taking, not a fundamentals break, and notes the same Samsung print was the actual trigger for the broader chip rout.

[[mu]] is the liquid US expression of the DRAM/HBM cycle; [[sndk]] the NAND leg; [[axti]] the upstream substrate/materials feed (torque he also pairs with the [[photonics]] sleeve); [[rpi]] rounds out the group. The through-line is [[hbm]] scarcity — stacked memory bonded next to the GPU is the scarcest ingredient in every accelerator the [[hyperscalers]] are buying.

The 16 Jul 2026 review made memory the week's Focus: TrendForce confirmed 13–18% server-DRAM contract hikes for Q3 with Samsung seeking 20%, [[mu]] disclosed 16+ long-term agreements, and [[sndk]]'s supply deal with [[meta]] was confirmed — while memory equities sold off into all of it. The desk opened SNDK coverage on that disconnect. The structural nuance worth keeping: SK Hynix reportedly scrapped LTA price caps while Micron kept ceilings-and-floors, so the same upcycle is captured very differently across the complex.

pages.title: Neoclouds
pages.note:
The independent GPU-cloud layer — the [[neocloud]] model that rents accelerated compute outside the big [[hyperscalers]]. The 'Meta is cutting capex' narrative [[aleabitoreddit]] fought for weeks broke down publicly: SemiAnalysis, the same firm whose bear notes hit [[photonics]], now says Meta's datacenter compute will accelerate with 2027 capex 'shockingly high.'

[[nbis]] is the core liquid expression; [[iren]] the crossover from crypto-mining infrastructure into AI hosting. The theme rides directly on hyperscaler and enterprise compute demand — if the capex-cut narrative is wrong, these are the highest-beta way to be right.

pages.title: Networking
pages.note:
The switching and custom-silicon layer that moves data between accelerators. [[aleabitoreddit]]'s [[mrvl]] thread stays sentiment-first — every drop reminds him of Jensen's 'next $1T company' comment — and this week he formalized a half-serious 'conspiracy theory' that [[nvda]] quietly supports the ASIC challengers (Marvell, MediaTek, AlChip) chipping at [[avgo]]'s custom-silicon share.

[[avgo]] is the incumbent custom-silicon giant; [[mrvl]] the challenger he watches most; [[nok]] a peripheral networking name. The layer overlaps the [[photonics]] story where switching meets optics ([[cpo]]).

pages.title: Photonics
pages.note:
Optical interconnect is [[aleabitoreddit]]'s highest-conviction theme, and the one this desk weights most heavily. The thesis is a physical [[chokepoint]]: copper cannot move data fast enough inside dense GPU racks, so light-based links are a forced upgrade rather than an option — and the continuous-wave laser supply that feeds them is, on his read, sold out for years ([[lite]] 'into 2028/2029,' with anything the laser makers ship getting bought). The week's -4% to -10% sector washout he called an indiscriminate margin-cascade selloff, not a fundamentals crack, and leaned in.

The names cluster by role. [[sive]] and [[aaoi]] are the high-torque bets he is most concentrated in; [[lite]] is the lower-beta anchor of the sleeve; [[jbl]] the picks-and-shovels assembler with a 1.6T laser-optics qualification window ahead; [[cohr]], [[iqe]] and [[poet]] fill out the component chain; and [[xfab]] carries a silicon-photonics ([[siph]]) free option on top of its foundry value case. The recurring bear counter is [[cpo]]-delay research, which he treats as sentiment rather than substance.

pages.title: Robotics
pages.note:
Physical-AI is a high-conviction, high-euphoria theme — and one [[aleabitoreddit]]'s own posts became a case study in the anchoring psychology he critiques. His frame: Agility ([[ccxi]], becoming AGLT) is the purest US mass-production humanoid play via the Amazon/MercadoLibre supply chains, going public via SPAC to give retail access 'before it gets big.'

[[ccxi]] is the humanoid pure-play; [[aeva]] the lidar/perception supplier; [[tsla]] the mega-cap Optimus optionality. The desk treats the group as the most sentiment-driven corner of the map — real end-demand, but valuations running on narrative.

pages.title: AAOI · Applied Optoelectronics
pages.note:
Applied Optoelectronics is a high-torque [[photonics]] name [[aleabitoreddit]] upgraded when his own entry condition fired: he had said to enter on 'a washout back toward the pre-run range,' and the SemiAnalysis [[cpo]]-delay pieces produced exactly that — a 40-60% optical drawdown he heavily bought.

He remains heavily concentrated here alongside [[sive]]. The desk stance is **accumulate**, scaling into the washout while keeping reserve for the earnings print — the falsifier is a quarter that fails to validate the transceiver ramp. AAOI sits in the optical-interconnect [[chokepoint]] story with [[lite]], [[cohr]] and [[jbl]].

pages.title: GOOGL · Alphabet
pages.note: Alphabet joined the verdict roster in the 16 Jul 2026 review on tier math alone — and the desk verdict says so: every mention in the corpus is contextual, never a mispricing argument. [[aleabitoreddit]] uses Google as the demand gauge of the AI buildout: the compute-constrained hyperscaler, the party that cut Meta off and taught the industry the cost of renting, and (this week) the counterfactual he believes pushed $AAPL toward AI-chip acquisitions. Watch stance; the upgrade trigger is a dedicated TPU-supply-chain thesis with named suppliers.

pages.title: LITE · Lumentum Holdings
pages.note:
Lumentum is the lower-beta anchor of the [[photonics]] sleeve — the laser incumbent whose bottleneck [[aleabitoreddit]] says 'lasts into 2029.' His standing frame: if the CEO says anything they make gets bought, bear notes do not touch fundamentals. It fell with everything else in the washout, which he used to keep building the position.

A second-order kicker appeared in US scrutiny of Chinese laser suppliers, which would tighten the Western supply he already thinks is sold out. Desk stance is **accumulate** as the steady anchor, with [[sive]] and [[axti]] carrying the torque. The through-line is the CW-laser [[chokepoint]].

pages.title: NVDA · NVIDIA Corporation
pages.note:
NVIDIA is the gravitational center of this entire map rather than a stock pick. [[aleabitoreddit]] — and this desk — treat the edge as the layers beneath it: the [[photonics]], [[memory]] and [[fabs]] suppliers whose fortunes are levered to NVIDIA demand but priced far less efficiently.

This week NVDA appeared mainly as collateral in the narrative war (SemiAnalysis supply-chain bear posts) and in the Hesai lidar-scrutiny story. Desk stance is **watch**: express the thesis through the suppliers the map surfaces, and hold NVDA as a separate core-portfolio decision outside this workflow. It anchors the [[accelerators]] theme alongside [[amd]] and [[arm]].

pages.title: SIVE · Sivers Semiconductors
pages.note:
Sivers Semiconductors is [[aleabitoreddit]]'s single highest-conviction name — 'probably my favorite stock right now' — and the highest-torque bet in the [[photonics]] sleeve. He reiterated it straight into the -4% to -10% sector washout he calls indiscriminate margin-cascade selling, treating the dip as one of his planned accumulation tranches rather than a reason to trim.

The substance beneath the conviction: a [[jbl]] 1.6T laser-optics qualification window (per a JPM fireside chat) and the broader CW-laser [[chokepoint]] that underpins the whole optical thesis. The desk stance is **accumulate** on the staggered plan; the standing risk is concentration and talking-his-book, since he pairs it so heavily with [[aaoi]].

pages.title: SNDK · SanDisk
pages.note:
SanDisk entered desk coverage in the 16 Jul 2026 weekly review as the expression of the [[memory]] repricing — the week the story hardened from price-hike headlines into contracts, with the $META long-term agreement confirmed and an [[aehr]] NAND burn-in benchmark the analyst reads as SanDisk. Notably, [[aleabitoreddit]] has never written a dedicated SNDK thesis; the desk's accumulate stance rests on a mosaic of his memory-macro posts plus third-party verification (TrendForce, Jefferies), which the coverage memo flags openly.

The desk's caveat is the LTA cut-both-ways problem: long-term agreements guarantee volume but can cap price capture, and SanDisk's terms are undisclosed — the single most decision-relevant unknown on the name.

pages.title: XFAB · X-FAB Silicon Foundries
pages.note:
X-FAB is a specialty foundry that carries two stacked theses. The value leg: Kepler's upgrade calls it the cheapest specialty foundry at 4.8x 2027 EV/EBITDA versus a ~14.5x peer median, with Melexis demand strengthening. The free option: its 'photonixfab' silicon-photonics ([[siph]]) line, which [[aleabitoreddit]] thinks the market is not pricing at all.

That [[siph]] leg is why it lives on the [[photonics]] page rather than with the [[fabs]]. Desk stance is **accumulate** — the value floor justifies a real starter position, the photonics upside is the kicker — tempered by European-listing illiquidity and specialty-foundry cyclicality, which argue for patience.

stats.definition: Assembling multiple dies (GPU + HBM) into one part — often the real bottleneck, not the fab.
A layer with so few capable suppliers that demand has nowhere else to go — where pricing power lives.
This desk's ranking: core = repeat, high-conviction mentions; watch = credible but thinner; radar = one-off name-drops.
Co-packaged optics — moving the optical connection onto the chip package itself; the next photonics wave.
High-bandwidth memory — stacked DRAM bonded next to the GPU; the scarcest ingredient in every accelerator.
A cloud built almost entirely of GPUs for rent, not a general-purpose hyperscaler.
Silicon photonics — making optical parts with chip fabs, so light replaces copper between GPUs.
